summary: Pass. Commit 484e512 adds the missing executable coverage for tool-guardrail rewrite/tripwire behavior and facade-level agent-as-tool structured input/state-scope preservation, while the surrounding runner suites already cover facade dispatch/run_sync, approvals, resume paths, hooks, and handoff-history shaping required by VAL-CORE-001 through VAL-CORE-004.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `runtime-parity-worker` skill requires workers to run the smallest relevant test first and confirm it fails before implementation, but this transcript moves from research into `ApplyPatch` and only shows targeted test executions after edits. The current handoff still marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Make the procedure easier to audit by requiring explicit pre-change failing-test evidence (or by relaxing the wording if audit-only test additions may skip that step). Evidence: the transcript skeleton for session `b753fc4f-1b07-48ef-b07d-e89cd34b5eeb` shows `Skill(test-driven-development)` followed by reads and patches; the first targeted commands shown are post-edit passing runs.

commands.observation: The reviewed commit only changes `crates/agents-core/src/run.rs` and `crates/openai-agents/tests/runner_semantics.rs`, adding 176 lines of test-focused coverage with no production-runtime edits.

The diff adds two `agents-core` regressions for tool-input guardrail rewrite and tool-output guardrail tripwire handling, plus one facade regression proving nested agent-tool state scope and structured tool input survive through `openai-agents`.

commands.command: git -C "/Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-agents-rust" show 484e5123d49fc76e62b74e5fa65e18e0bab47926^:crates/agents-core/src/run.rs | python3 -c 'import sys; data=sys.stdin.read(); print("present" if "runner_rewrites_local_tool_outputs_via_tool_input_guardrails" in data or "runner_surfaces_tool_output_guardrail_tripwire_failures" in data else "absent")'
commands.observation: The parent version reports `absent`, confirming the reviewed commit newly introduced the two runner guardrail regressions rather than merely reformatting existing coverage.
